calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing And Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Administrative Mandamus
Off Calendar Dec-12-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Rose Darling, Deputy City Attorney, 415-554-3959 of the Office of the Attorney's Office for Defendant CCSF.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER SANDRA FUNES' Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Administrative Mandamus.

Petitioner Funes petition for writ of administrative mandamus is denied. Substantial evidence supports respondent's conclusion that petitioner's EEO complaints were untimely. "Substantial evidence" is defined as evidence of "ponderable legal significance, reasonable in nature, credible, and of solid value [and] relevant evidence that a reasonable mind might accept as adequate to support a conclusion." (Young v. Gannon (2002) 97 Cal.App.4th 209, 225.) The parties agree that "[u]nder the City's internal complaint procedures, complaints of discrimination must be filed within 180 calendar days of the date the allege discriminatory action took place or the date the employee would have first become aware of the discriminatory action." (AR 77.) Petitioner contends that her first EEO complaint filed on 1/31/17 was timely because she learned of discriminatory acts regarding her co-workers in 11/16. The record, however, indicates that petitioner could have filed her EEO complaint within 180 days of her reinstatement denial, which occurred on 4/13/16. Petitioner filed a DFEH complaint regarding the same incidents on 9/20/16 and nothing prevented her from seeking relief before respondent during the 180-day period. (AR 68-70; 74.) Petitioner fails to show that respondent's failure to waive the limitations period was arbitrary or an abuse of discretion.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/HEK)
